Field Identification Key · 18 Species

Turtles of Louisiana

Interactive dichotomous key — answer questions step by step to identify any turtle you encounter

Identify Your Turtle

This key guides you through a series of binary choices — each question narrows down the possibilities until you reach a species identification. Louisiana has 18 regularly occurring turtle species, from tiny musk turtles to the massive Alligator Snapping Turtle. Observe shell shape, keel pattern, head markings, and habitat carefully before starting.

All Louisiana turtles are native. Snapping turtles can deliver a painful bite on land and should never be picked up by the tail. The Alligator Snapping Turtle, Ringed Map Turtle, Sabine Map Turtle, and Gopher Tortoise are species of conservation concern — do not collect or disturb them.
